candidates.direction_name: Collapse The Compact PTX Wait-Sync Seam Into A Pairwise Stage Advance
ranking_notes: The restored anchor proved the wide-tiling loss is gone, so the next high-signal move is to attack the compact loop's explicit wait/sync seam directly.
This family already has positive history in the search tree, but it has not been sampled from the current restored anchor.
It preserves the winning 22,016 B shared-memory surface and avoids mixing another geometry change into the same round.
candidates.hypothesis: The restored compact PTX loop still waits and synchronizes on every next_tile boundary before deciding whether a future refill exists. Recasting that handoff into a pairwise stage advance should reduce barrier and scoreboard pressure together without reopening the high-shared 256x128 loss surface.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier and long-scoreboard are the clearest remaining compact-surface bottlenecks on the restored anchor.
candidates.risk: Moderate. This stays on the compact PTX hot loop, but it directly touches the synchronization seam that guards the async-copy pipeline.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the pairwise advance requires extra hot-loop predicates or any third shared-memory stage, or if correctness fails on the first buildable attempt.
expected_local_changes: Refactor the compact PTX hot loop so wait/sync advances in pairwise stages instead of on every single next_tile boundary.
Keep grouped_rows=4, 128x128 CTA geometry, and the 22,016 B shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Do not mix this experiment with a tiling or export-path change.

candidates.direction_name: Trim The Compact PTX Wait Group And Sync Cadence Without Growing Shared Memory
ranking_notes: This is the lower-disruption fallback if the pairwise collapse looks too invasive in code review.
It keeps the compact PTX surface intact and only adjusts the timing of the existing wait/sync seam.
It ranks second because a true pairwise stage advance has a better chance of reducing both barrier and scoreboard together.
candidates.hypothesis: If the pairwise collapse proves too invasive, a narrower retime of wait_group and __syncthreads placement on the restored compact surface can still attack barrier without reopening the shared-memory or geometry risks from recent failures.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier handoff overhead is still large enough to justify a smaller cadence-only probe on the compact PTX surface.
candidates.risk: Moderate. This is narrower than dir_01, but it still edits fragile synchronization placement in the hot loop.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the trim increases long-scoreboard or forces any shared-memory growth beyond the current 22,016 B footprint.
expected_local_changes: Retime wait_group and __syncthreads placement inside the compact PTX loop without changing stage depth.
Keep grouped_rows=4, the 128x128 dispatch, and the shared-memory budget unchanged.
Avoid pairing this with any pointer-hoist or tiling change.

candidates.direction_name: Keep The 2-K-Stage Pg2s Port Parked Behind The Compact Sync Experiments
ranking_notes: This family stays in the tree because it still targets the right high-level problem, but it has lower priority after the recent shared-budget loss evidence.
It ranks behind both compact sync experiments because those are cleaner and cheaper on the restored anchor.
Only revisit it if the compact wait/sync families flatten out again.
candidates.hypothesis: The 2-K-stage Pg2s port may still amortize waits, but after the failed 256x128 reopen it should remain behind the compact sync families unless the implementation can clearly avoid replaying the large-shared-budget failure mode.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: If revisited later, the target is still compact-surface latency hiding and copy cadence, not a new geometry surface.
candidates.risk: High. This family still threatens shared-memory growth and a worse residency regime unless it is very carefully constrained.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the port requires a larger shared-memory surface or drops the compact anchor below the current two-CTA register-limited regime.
expected_local_changes: Keep the compact 128x128 PTX dispatch active.
Only port the 2-K-stage preload/drain structure if the shared-memory budget remains tightly controlled.
Avoid combining the port with any tiling or launch-order change.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
